message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>We predict the evolution of the COVID-19 pandemic in several countries by using a regression-based predictive model. In particular, the growth rate of the infection has been fitted as an exponential decay, as compared to a linear decay, reported previously. The model has been validated with the data of China and South Korea, where the pandemic is nearing to its end. The data of Italy, Germany, Spain, and Sweden show that the peak of the infection has reached i.e. a time when the new infections will start to decrease as compared to the previous day. The model predicts the approximate number of total infections at the end of the outbreak. The model prediction of the USA, and Brazil show that the peak will reach in the next two-three weeks. The total number of infections in the USA is estimated to be around 4 million by the model. The reported data of India show the start of disease evolution, with a large initial scatter in the growth rate. The possible peak date and the total number of infections are predicted using the data available.</jats:p>
title: A Predictive Model for the Evolution of COVID-19

reference.journal-title: Predicting the ultimate outcome of the COVID-19 outbreak in Italy
Age-structured impact of social distancing on the COVID-19 epidemic in India

reference.unstructured: Mahendra K. Verma , Ali Asad , Soumyadeep Chatterjee, COVID-19 epidemic: Power law spread and flat-tening of the curve, medRxiv 2020.04.02.20051680, doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.04.02.20051680 2020.
